articles.title: UN commission of inquiry says Israel committing genocide in Gaza by deliberately targeting children
articles.summary: A three-member UN independent commission of inquiry said in a report released Tuesday that Israeli authorities and forces have deliberately targeted Palestinian children, amounting to genocide and atrocity crimes in Gaza and war crimes in the West Bank. The panel said at least 20,179 children were killed between October 2023 and October 2025 — about 30 percent of the overall death toll — and that continued use of high-payload, wide-area munitions in dense residential areas indicated the killings were intentional. It also cited attacks on neonatal and maternity care and a sharp rise in settler violence and detention abuses in the West Bank. Israel rejected the findings, with its Geneva mission calling it a 'second defamatory advocacy report' and a 'libellous sham'.

articles.title: Israeli troops kill two in south Lebanon after lull in fighting, authorities say
articles.summary: Lebanese authorities said Israeli troops killed two men in southern Lebanon after several days of relative calm. Israel's military said the two were Hezbollah operatives, while the Iran-backed group accused Israel of violating the US-brokered ceasefire. The incident underscored the fragility of the truce reached last week as Israel and Hezbollah have continued sporadic exchanges despite the agreement to halt fighting.

articles.title: Dozens of ships transit Strait of Hormuz as more vessels venture through, but the waterway's future stays unsettled
articles.summary: Maritime tracking data showed dozens of ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz in the days after the US-Iran deal — including 42 vessels on Saturday — though traffic remained well below pre-war levels. Pakistan's Deputy PM and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar said the agreement envisages free passage during the 60-day negotiating window with no fees, while analysts cited lingering obstacles of security, sea mines and potential tolls. The strait's long-term status remains a core unresolved question in the broader settlement.

articles.title: Iran says no new commitments on nuclear sites after Vance says inspectors to be invited back
articles.summary: Iran's foreign ministry said it made 'no new commitments' on nuclear inspections following talks in Switzerland, directly contradicting VP JD Vance's claim that Tehran had agreed to readmit IAEA inspectors to damaged nuclear sites. Iranian officials said cooperation with the UN watchdog would continue only under existing procedures and that Iran alone would determine the matter, while Vance said there was a 'good foundation' for a final deal. The conflicting statements highlighted how much of the emerging framework remains unsettled.

articles.title: Iran's chief negotiator Ghalibaf says Strait of Hormuz will be administered by Tehran
articles.summary: Iran's chief negotiator Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf said the Strait of Hormuz 'will never return to its pre-war conditions and will be administered by the Islamic Republic of Iran, in accordance with international law,' according to state media. The assertion of Iranian control over the strategic waterway sits awkwardly alongside US and Pakistani statements that the US-Iran deal guarantees free commercial passage, signalling continued friction over how the strait will be governed.

articles.title: Israel-Lebanon direct talks wrap up in Washington after eight hours, to resume at Pentagon
articles.summary: Direct talks between Israel and Lebanon concluded in Washington after more than eight hours covering both political and security issues, with sessions set to resume Wednesday at the Pentagon for security-focused discussions before returning to the State Department on Thursday. The negotiations run in parallel to the US-Iran track and the fragile Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire. Separately, the IDF said it captured a Hezbollah drone factory and launch site hidden inside a south Lebanon mountain.

articles.title: Israeli government funds plan to turn Palestinian archaeological site into settler facility
articles.summary: Far-right Knesset member Zvi Sukkot announced a project to convert the Fasayel Pools archaeological site in the occupied Jordan Valley into a tourist resort and swimming area for settlers, backed by 3 million shekels in direct government funding allocated by Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich. Critics say the move is part of a wider Israeli policy of taking control of Palestinian heritage sites and rebranding them with Jewish or biblical significance. The report came amid wider settler activity in the area, including the destruction of water and irrigation networks near Tubas.

articles.title: Trump says anyone in Congress critical of Iran deal 'has to be educated'
articles.summary: President Donald Trump pushed back on congressional criticism of the US-Iran memorandum of understanding, saying anyone on Capitol Hill critical of the deal 'has to be educated' on its merits. The remarks came as the administration works to sell the framework to skeptical lawmakers and Gulf allies, with the agreement's nuclear provisions and the $300bn redevelopment package for Iran drawing scrutiny.
